mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) is a robust indicator of consumer demand strength, which directly supports corporate revenue and earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If over any rolling 3-year window, positive RSAFS growth is associated with negative median 20-day forward returns, or the rank correlation between RSAFS growth and forward returns is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the unemployment rate above its 3-month moving average by more than 0.2 percentage points signals a deterioration in labor market conditions that reduces consumer spending power and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If triggered instances produce positive median 20-day returns over at least 60% of occurrences, or the signal fails to have a negative average return over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) experience positive month-over-month growth simultaneously, it confirms a synchronized expansion in both supply and demand sides of the economy, boosting confidence and corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both INDPRO and RSAFS MoM change positive
failure_condition: If during months with both series rising, the median 20-day forward return is not significantly above the unconditional median (bootstrapped confidence interval includes zero), or the signal is negative more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: During identified monetary tightening cycles, defined as periods when the federal funds rate is above its 12-month moving average and has increased over the previous month, further rate increases raise discount rates and compress equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If within tightening cycles identified, the median 60-day forward return is not negative or the proportion of negative returns is less than 60% over a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A low level of the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates tight labor markets that support consumer income, spending, and corporate profits, thereby producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the median 5-day forward return in the lowest UNRATE quartile is not higher than the median in the highest quartile, or the Spearman rank correlation between UNRATE and returns is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate of at least 25 basis points signals an emergency easing response to severe economic stress, which raises default risk concerns and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in instances where FEDFUNDS drops by 0.25 or more, the median 20-day forward return is positive, or the proportion of negative returns is less than 65% over all such events.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and decrease in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) provides a comprehensive confirmation of labor market strengthening, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM increase and UNRATE MoM decrease
failure_condition: If the median 20-day forward return following such joint signals is not greater than the unconditional median, or the probability of positive return is less than 60% over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, identified when both real retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) are above their 12-month moving averages but month-over-month growth is decelerating (slowing), this indicates maturing expansion without acceleration, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both series above 12-month MA and MoM growth positive but declining
failure_condition: If during late-cycle regimes defined by these criteria, the median 60-day forward return is positive, or the proportion of negative returns is less than 55% over a rolling 10-year window.
